exercises.title: Trace @ValueSource execution count
exercises.scenario: @ValueSource creates one test execution per value.
hints: One execution per value in the array
Default name: [index] value

exercises.title: Basic @CsvSource test
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete this parameterized test.

blanks.hint: Third column represents the expected result

hints: @ParameterizedTest triggers the test framework to run with multiple inputs
@CsvSource '3, 4, 7' → a=3, b=4, expected=7

exercises.title: ValueSource for positive/negative
exercises.scenario: Test valid and invalid inputs with @ValueSource.
exercises.instructions:
Write two parameterized tests for `BankAccount.deposit(double amount)`:
1. @ValueSource(doubles) with valid amounts — assertDoesNotThrow
2. @ValueSource(doubles) with invalid amounts — assertThrows

Valid: 1, 100, 999.99
Invalid: 0, -1, -100
hints: @ValueSource(doubles = {...}) for double parameters
assertDoesNotThrow(() -> call()) for the happy path

exercises.title: @CsvSource with multiple parameters
exercises.scenario: CsvSource maps each CSV row to test parameters.
exercises.instructions:
Write a parameterized test for a discount calculator using @CsvSource:
- qty=1 → 0%
- qty=10 → 10%
- qty=50 → 20%
- qty=9 → 0%
- qty=49 → 10%
hints: @CsvSource({"1, 0.00", ...}) — each string is one CSV row
assertEquals with delta for doubles: assertEquals(0.10, result, 0.001)

exercises.title: @NullAndEmptySource for null/empty handling
exercises.scenario: Test that methods handle null and empty inputs gracefully.
exercises.instructions:
Write a parameterized test using @NullAndEmptySource that verifies `ProductSearch.find(String query)` returns an empty list for null and empty inputs (not throws).

Expected: 2 executions (null and ""), both pass.
hints: @NullAndEmptySource runs twice: null, then empty string
assertNotNull(result) — list should never be null, even for null query

exercises.title: @EnumSource for all payment methods
exercises.instructions:
Write a test that verifies `PaymentProcessor.getFee(PaymentMethod)` returns a non-negative value for ALL payment methods.

Enum: CARD, UPI, NET_BANKING, WALLET

Expected: 4 executions, all pass.
hints: @EnumSource(PaymentMethod.class) iterates all enum values
assertTrue with message helps identify which enum value failed

exercises.title: Boundary value testing — quantity limits
exercises.scenario: Test all boundaries for a 1-999 quantity range.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @CsvSource parameterized test covering ALL boundaries for `OrderValidator.validateQty(int qty)` which throws on qty < 1 or qty > 999.

Test: 0, 1, 2, 998, 999, 1000 → valid/invalid
hints: Test n-1 (0), n (1), n+1 (2) at lower boundary
Test m-1 (998), m (999), m+1 (1000) at upper boundary
Use assertDoesNotThrow for valid inputs

exercises.title: @MethodSource for complex Product objects
exercises.scenario: MethodSource provides complex objects as parameters.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @MethodSource test for `ProductValidator.isValid(Product product)` that accepts a stream of valid and invalid Products.

Provide 5 products: 3 valid, 2 invalid. Test returns true/false.
hints: static Stream<Arguments> methodName() { return Stream.of(...) }
Arguments.of(object1, object2) maps to test parameters
Method must be static and return Stream

exercises.title: Combined @NullAndEmptySource + @ValueSource
exercises.scenario: Test all blank inputs with multiple annotations.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test that verifies `UserService.createUser(String name, int age)` throws IllegalArgumentException for blank names (null, empty, space).

Use @NullAndEmptySource + @ValueSource(strings = {' ', '   '}).
hints: Stack @NullAndEmptySource and @ValueSource on the same test method
Total executions: 2 (NullAndEmpty) + 2 (ValueSource) = 4

exercises.title: @CsvSource with empty string syntax
exercises.scenario: Empty strings in CsvSource need single-quote wrapping.
hints: Row 1: name = 'Mouse' (simple)
Row 2: single quotes wrap → 'Dell 27, Monitor' (comma inside)
Row 3: '' (two single quotes) → empty string
Row 4: nothing before comma → null

exercises.title: GST rate calculator — boundary testing
exercises.scenario: Tax slabs need thorough boundary coverage.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @CsvSource parameterized test for `GSTCalculator.getRate(double price)` with slabs:
- price < 1000 → 5%
- price < 5000 → 12%
- price < 15000 → 18%
- price >= 15000 → 28%

Test at least 8 values covering all boundaries and slab midpoints.
hints: Three boundaries: 1000, 5000, 15000
Each boundary: test value-1, value, value+1
Plus one midpoint per slab

exercises.title: @MethodSource with multiple argument types
exercises.scenario: MethodSource Arguments.of() maps to multiple parameters.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @MethodSource test for `StockPosition.classify()` which returns 'WINNER', 'LOSER', or 'FLAT' based on P&L percentage.

Provide 5 test cases as Arguments.of(symbol, shares, buyPrice, curPrice, expectedClass).
hints: Arguments.of(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5) maps to test method parameters
5 args: symbol, shares, buyPrice, curPrice, expectedClass

exercises.title: Comprehensive validator test with all sources
exercises.scenario: Use all four @Source types in one test class.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test class for `EmailValidator.isValid(String email)` using:
1. @ValueSource(strings) for valid emails
2. @NullAndEmptySource + @ValueSource for blank inputs → false
3. @CsvSource for edge cases: no @, multiple @, valid with subdomain
4. @MethodSource for complex valid/invalid examples with descriptions
hints: Four separate @ParameterizedTest methods, each with different @Source
@CsvSource with 'aisha@sub.example.co.in' — single quotes for dot in field
@MethodSource with 3rd param as description → passed as third method arg

exercises.title: Pricing engine boundary tests
exercises.scenario: Multi-rule pricing engine with complex boundaries.
exercises.instructions:
Write comprehensive boundary tests for `PricingEngine.finalPrice(int qty, double unitPrice, boolean premiumUser)`:
- Qty discount: qty<10→0%, qty<50→10%, qty>=50→20%
- Premium user: extra 5% off after qty discount
- Minimum charge: ₹10 even if computed total < ₹10

Write @CsvSource with at least 10 cases covering all rules and boundaries.
hints: Minimum charge: 1 * 1 = 1; max(1, 10) = 10
Premium: 5% off AFTER qty discount

exercises.title: Complete parameterized test suite for CSV parser
exercises.scenario: Exhaustive edge case coverage using all parameterized techniques.
exercises.instructions:
Write a full parameterized test suite for `CSVParser.parseCSVLine(String)` (the Topic 11.3 state machine):

1. @CsvSource for normal inputs: field count and first field value
2. @MethodSource for complex quoted cases with expected field lists
3. @NullAndEmptySource — null/empty string handling
4. @ValueSource(strings) for single-field lines
5. Boundary: maximum field count (test with 1, 2, 10 fields)

At least 15 total test executions.
hints: @CsvSource: use single quotes to embed commas in CSV param value
@MethodSource: return Stream<Arguments> of (lineString, List<String>)
Total: 4+4+2+4 = 14 executions
